description_long: This bungalow features a pitched gable roof with blue-gray shingles, set atop a rectangular structure with tan stucco walls and dark wooden beams. The front facade includes a small wooden porch with steps leading up to a covered entryway, flanked by railings and supported by posts. Windows are arranged in pairs on the side walls, each with deep wooden frames, while the rear of the house has a single door and a small green utility box. The building sits on a grassy plot with patches of dirt and a small pond nearby, suggesting a suburban or semi-rural setting. The roof overhangs provide shade and weather protection, and the overall design is simple yet functional. The materials and construction evoke a traditional, craftsman-inspired style suitable for residential neighborhoods.
description: A tan stucco bungalow with a blue-gray gable roof, wooden porch, and paired side windows, set on a grassy plot with a small pond.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, steps, and covered entryway with visible wooden beams.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting paired windows and the full length of the gable roof, with the pond visible to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a single door, a small green utility box, and the plain back wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the porch and steps most prominent, and the pond on the right.
attribute_window_arrangement: paired side windows, single rear window
